Description: Total Households; The total count of all household units in the administrative area.
General Households; Dwelling units where a family or group of persons live and eat together.
Institutional Households; Groups living in hostels, hospitals, jails, or barracks.
Other Households; "Floating" populations or people living in non-dwelling structures (e.g., shops, offices).
Home Owned (Total); Number of households living in a dwelling owned by a member of that household.
Home Rented (Total); Number of households living in a dwelling for which they pay monthly/periodic rent.
Rent-Free (Total); Number of households living in a dwelling without paying any rent (e.g., provided by relatives or employers).
Number of males/females currently attending school.
Individuals primary engaged in domestic work.
Individuals not engaged in any work.
Number of people employed in Agriculture.
Number of people employed in Industry/Manufacturing.
Number of people employed in the Service sector.
% of houses made of concrete/permanent materials.
% of houses made of temporary materials (mud/straw).
% of households with hygienic water-sealed toilets.
% of households with access to tap water
% of households that own their dwelling
Extreme poverty status rank (descending by status - higher numerica rank indicating lower status)
Multidimensional Poverty Index at district level.
Normalized level of migration out of the area.
Risk of water depletion or salinity/arsenic.
Index of population historically affected by disasters.
Risk to agricultural output due to climate.
The percentage change in population between 2011 and 2022.
Total number of population under each religion

Column Name: People Affected Due to Natural Disaster
Ground Water Quality Depletion and Degradation
Decrease in Livestock & Poultry Health Vulnerability
Missing Values: Columns like Mun_Name and Mun_CVI have high null counts (approx 258) because they only apply to "Pourashavas" (Municipalities), not all Upazilas.
Normalization: Columns ending in _N (e.g., MPI_N, HCR Upper-22_N) are normalized values used for comparative analysis.
Muslim, Hindu, Christian, Buddhist, Others: Absolute counts of individuals belonging to these religions in each Upazila.
